snippet.textDisplay:
I appreciate this video, and you seem to be the only expert on the internet who actually tests these things, but what about the 500-1000 range? 
The sub 500 ones all have pretty glaring downsides, while the 1000+ one are just too much for what I want it to do. 
Information about projectors in that range is very bad, with people only recommending projectors that only exist on US 2nd hand marketplaces. 

Are there no good projectors in that range, or what is the reason why there's almost zero information?
snippet.textOriginal:

snippet.textDisplay:
You are incorrect as “bedside” stands.  

I’ve got the Epson EF72. Although I am satisfied with the image and sound quality as a bedroom projector. I has a major downside. This is that while setting the projector on a nightstand it can’t project an image centered on the ceiling above your two-persons bed!

You can only achieve that when you place a thick book or some wooden blocks underneath the stand tilting the entire machine towards the bed!
snippet.textOriginal:
You are incorrect as “bedside” stands.  

I’ve got the Epson EF72. Although I am satisfied with the image and sound quality as a bedroom projector. I has a major downside. This is that while setting the projector on a nightstand it can’t project an image centered on the ceiling above your two-persons bed!

You can only achieve that when you place a thick book or some wooden blocks underneath the stand tilting the entire machine towards the bed!

snippet.textDisplay:
Alright, Bought a projector in this price class based on your recommendation.
I was trying to keep it around a grand.

I set out to buy the xgimi Horizon 20 hoping to get a deal.   Unfortunately , the price has gone up to about $1450.  Surprisingly, your number two pick on the list was down to $999 from $1499 at time of your video.  Amazon even had used like-new ones for $899. The last several times I took that option they sent me new stuff.  So I gambled and bought it.  Can't wait to try it.  I've never had a nice projector before.  My current one is a yaber k1.
snippet.textOriginal:
Alright, Bought a projector in this price class based on your recommendation.

snippet.textDisplay:
Love the reviews and all the data. Purchased the Horizon 20 and returned it 3 days later. The laser speckle on my Silver Ticket 1.0 gain screen was really bad. Went back to my 2021 Nebula Cosmos 4k. 1100 lumens. I feel like lumens are overrated. The horizon 20 was brighter but not as much as I would expect. 

I did not want to invest in a new screen that handles the laser better. 

Starting to think the projectors have a strong diminishing returns.
snippet.textOriginal:
Love the reviews and all the data. Purchased the Horizon 20 and returned it 3 days later. The laser speckle on my Silver Ticket 1.0 gain screen was really bad. Went back to my 2021 Nebula Cosmos 4k. 1100 lumens. I feel like lumens are overrated. The horizon 20 was brighter but not as much as I would expect. 

I did not want to invest in a new screen that handles the laser better. 

Starting to think the projectors have a strong diminishing returns.

snippet.textDisplay:
Hi, I received my Xgimi horizon 20, ive been testing doing a little tweak to colors etc.

So I see the picture washed out from sdr, hdr and dolby vision content, no matter what I do to fix it , its like a sensation of a foggy image. 

I was expecting to be blown away by this Horizon 20 because all of the specs on paper.

I have a Dangei Atom and the colors are lively and pop up way more.

Is it something that im doing wrong with the settings? Please if you could help me out with this issue, thanks

One last thing the enhanced black level does not engage when switchig to on and off, not even turning purple like your video.
snippet.textOriginal:
Hi, I received my Xgimi horizon 20, ive been testing doing a little tweak to colors etc.

So I see the picture washed out from sdr, hdr and dolby vision content, no matter what I do to fix it , its like a sensation of a foggy image. 

I was expecting to be blown away by this Horizon 20 because all of the specs on paper.

I have a Dangei Atom and the colors are lively and pop up way more.

Is it something that im doing wrong with the settings? Please if you could help me out with this issue, thanks

One last thing the enhanced black level does not engage when switchig to on and off, not even turning purple like your video.

snippet.textDisplay: Could you do a double take on the dB output for the JmGO? Other reviews state two 10w speakers same as the m2 pro. Hard to believe it is 3.5x the perceived loudness.
snippet.textOriginal: Could you do a double take on the dB output for the JmGO? Other reviews state two 10w speakers same as the m2 pro. Hard to believe it is 3.5x the perceived loudness.

snippet.textDisplay:
I received my Hisense M2 Pro today, and although I'm used to very good picture quality from two OLEDs, including an LG 77 C2, I'm very satisfied with the picture quality.

In fact, it's even too bright in a completely dark room; I had to turn the brightness down considerably.

The only noise I notice is the fan; the projector is directly above my head on the bed. It's like a faint whirring sound. I'm worried it will bother me when I'm watching movies.

My question is: based on the video, the Xgimi Horizon 20 sounds a bit quieter (more of a whoosh than a whir). Is that correct? And is it worth spending over €700 more just for that? (German prices)
I don't need the increased brightness.
snippet.textOriginal:

snippet.textDisplay:
I really love your Videos!

And I have a question 🤣
Right now the JMGO N1S Ultra is in sale for 1300€.The Horizon for 1450€. 
Arent These two more comparable than the comparison with the Small JMGo N1 S?

I am going to use it for Movies Most of the time, but if I want to Game with Friends on my Couch I dont want an annoying Input lag.

The small JMGo N1 S in this Video has a good Input lag, But the bigger one  JMGO N1S Ultra has a very Bad Input lag in one of your older Videos. How does that come?

Which one should I get?😅

snippet.textDisplay: Thank you! Now subscribed!  I already had the XGIMI in cart - even less $s now for BF on amazon.  Upgrading from Panasonic PT-AE8000U - so big jump.  What I still need, if you know of one - is a simple (hold position) HDMI splitter = 1 in and 2 out, where the switch HOLDs its position regardless of inputs into the source AV receiver (HDMI output, all individual video/audio sources input to AV receiver, surround sound Bose 5.1 AV Receiver audio output); the splitter I have works but reverts to the first out position (TV) when any source changes (and shrinks the image to a fraction = forces multiple devices resets to get corrected).  So, thank you - this solves my research and I will get the XGIMI, just need a MUCH better HDMI splitter (TV vs Projector). HAPPY GOBBLE!
snippet.textOriginal: Thank you! Now subscribed!  I already had the XGIMI in cart - even less $s now for BF on amazon.  Upgrading from Panasonic PT-AE8000U - so big jump. So, thank you - this solves my research and I will get the XGIMI, just need a MUCH better HDMI splitter (TV vs Projector). HAPPY GOBBLE!
